question: Will Mara Voss's scheduling copilot reach a committed public launch or at least three signed design partners by mid-September 2025?

unmodeled_actor_action.description: schema-scoped scaffolding: an actor action whose semantics could not be compiled; exact content preserved; counted as a fallback

launch_budget_cap.rule: mara_voss can spend up to 40k without board approval; any amount over 50k requires board majority approval
launch_budget_cap.evidence: Mara holds 40k discretionary launch budget; board must approve launch spend over 50k

pilot_decision_timeline.rule: Devon Reyes reports pilot expansion decision in approximately 3 weeks from prediction time (2025-07-18), i.e., by ~2025-08-08
pilot_decision_timeline.evidence: Devon Reyes runs the enterprise pilot at Calder Logistics, decides pilot expansion, reports in ~3 weeks

mara_voss_discretionary_budget.constraints: can be used for launch activities up to 40k total

board_approval_required_budget.constraints: any launch spend exceeding 50k requires board majority approval

board_decision_visibility.rule: board_decision_record is visible to all board members and CEO after recording

pilot_decision_visibility.rule: pilot_expansion_decision is visible to mara_voss and devon_reyes immediately

design_partner_signing_visibility.rule: signed_design_partner fact is public upon signing event

mara_voss.why_consequential: controls discretionary launch budget up to 40k; drives design partner acquisition; can initiate launch commitment
affordances: spend up to 40k on launch activities without board approval
negotiate and sign design partner agreements
call board meeting for launch spend approval

devon_reyes.why_consequential: decides whether pilot expands, which could serve as a design partner signal or revenue source

priya_shah.why_consequential: chairs the 3-seat board that must approve launch spend over 50k; holds a vote

third_board_member_unknown.why_consequential: holds the third vote needed for majority decisions

unresolved_mechanisms: identity and voting preference of the third board member is unknown
exact launch cost and whether it will exceed 50k is unknown
whether mara_voss will seek board approval or stay within 40k discretionary budget is unknown
specific design partner prospects and their negotiation timelines are unknown
assumptions: board operates by simple majority (2 of 3 votes) for launch spend approval
board_decision_record is created for each vote and is authoritative
pilot_expansion_decision by devon_reyes is binary (expand/terminate) and reported within 3 weeks
design partner agreements require a signed contract to count
mid-September 2025 horizon means any commitment or signing on or before 2025-09-16 counts

auto_repairs: institution 'seed_investor_board' aggregation defaulted to majority (none declared; labeled assumed)

missing_decisive_elements: No representation of Mara Voss's personal financial runway or external funding timeline that could force or block launch regardless of board approval
No representation of competitor product launches or market timing pressures that could accelerate or delay commitment
No representation of technical readiness milestones (e.g., beta completion, bug count, integration testing) that are prerequisite to public launch
No representation of legal/regulatory hurdles (e.g., data privacy compliance, contract review cycles) that could block signing or launch
No representation of the 'third board member unknown' identity or their incentives/constraints, making the board vote outcome fundamentally unmodelable
No representation of Calder Logistics' internal procurement or legal approval process that Devon Reyes must navigate before signing any design partner agreement
relabeled_progress_bars: The 'design_partner_acquisition_process' has only four states but no branching for multiple simultaneous prospects, no state for 'contract sent for legal review', and no dead-end state for 'prospect went silent'
The 'launch_approval_process' assumes board review is triggered only by spend >40k, but ignores that a public launch commitment itself may require board approval regardless of spend amount
The 'pilot_expansion_process' treats Devon Reyes as a unitary decider, but in real enterprises, pilot expansion decisions involve multiple stakeholders (IT, procurement, legal) not captured
hidden_answer_risks: The model assumes 'signed design partner' is a binary event, but real agreements often have conditional clauses, phased rollouts, or non-binding LOIs that may not count as 'signed'
The model treats 'public launch committed' as a single event, but a company could announce a 'soft launch' or 'beta launch' that is publicly committed but not a full public launch, creating ambiguity
The model has no mechanism for Mara Voss to 'fail' — no bankruptcy, no founder departure, no loss of key engineer — all of which are common in startups
The model assumes board decisions are made by majority vote, but real boards often operate by consensus or defer to the chair, especially in early-stage startups
direct_human_reaction_encodings: Mara Voss's 'affordances' list includes 'announce public launch date' but no constraint that such an announcement would be reckless without board approval or technical readiness — this conflates authority with capability
Devon Reyes's affordances list 'report decision to mara_voss' but no encoding of how long that reporting takes or whether it can be delayed
Priya Shah's affordances include 'influence other board members' but no mechanism for how that influence works (e.g., persuasion, voting bloc, quid pro quo)
public_posture_vs_private_reality_confla: The model treats 'public_launch_committed' as a factual record, but a company can publicly commit to a launch date while privately knowing it will slip — the model has no way to represent private doubts or internal reforecasts

The model treats 'signed_design_partner' as a clean fact, but a partner may sign publicly for PR while privately having no intention to actually use the product — the model conflates signature with genuine partnership
missing_nonhuman_mechanisms: No representation of software development velocity (e.g., sprint completion rate, feature freeze dates) that determines whether the product is shippable
No representation of cloud infrastructure costs or server capacity that could delay launch if not budgeted
No representation of external dependencies like API providers, third-party data sources, or hardware vendors that could block launch
No representation of calendar effects (e.g., August vacation slowdown, holiday season) that could delay signing or launch by mid-September
